summary:
Retinol Binding Protein 4 (RBP4) is primarily associated with vitamin A transportation and not derived from adipose tissue. It lacks definitive classification as a classic adipokine due to absence of proven origin or hormonal function linked directly to fat cell secretion. 

Final Answer: \boxed{\text{No}}

Retinol binding protein 4 (RBP4) functions mainly in vitamin A transport and originates outside adipose tissue. It has not been confirmed as being produced by adipocytes or acting like other defined adipokines despite associations with glucose metabolism anomalies.  

Final Answer: $\boxed{\text{No}}$
